agreement_structures.section_description: Define key terms including Confidential Information, Disclosing Party, Receiving Party, Representatives, and Purpose. The definition of Confidential Information is the most heavily negotiated provision.
Core duty to maintain confidentiality, standard of care (at least same care as for own confidential information, and in any event reasonable care), and restrictions on use (solely for the Purpose).
Standard carve-outs: publicly available information, prior possession, independent development, third-party receipt without restriction. Burden of proof rests on the Receiving Party.
Circumstances in which disclosure is allowed: to Representatives on a need-to-know basis, compelled by law or court order (with notice obligation), and to professional advisors.
Obligation to return or destroy Confidential Information upon request or termination. Include certification requirement and archival copy exception for regulatory compliance.
Duration of the agreement (during which new disclosures may occur) and survival period for confidentiality obligations after termination. Trade secret obligations survive indefinitely.
Acknowledgment that breach may cause irreparable harm. Right to seek equitable relief (injunction, specific performance) in addition to damages. Cumulative remedies.

agreement_structures.section_name: No Obligation to Disclose or Deal
agreement_structures.section_description: Reservation that neither party is obligated to disclose any particular information or to enter into any further agreement or transaction.
Choice of governing law and submission to jurisdiction. Consider carve-out allowing equitable relief in any competent court.
Boilerplate: entire agreement, amendment (in writing only), waiver, severability, notices, assignment restrictions, counterparts.
Define Disclosing Party, Receiving Party, Confidential Information (broadly drafted to protect the Disclosing Party), and Purpose. In a unilateral NDA, only one party discloses.
Core confidentiality duty, standard of care, use restrictions, and non-disclosure obligations. More detailed than mutual NDA because only one party bears obligations.
Standard exclusions: publicly available, prior possession, independent development, third-party receipt. Same as mutual NDA.
Limited circumstances for authorized disclosure. Typically narrower than mutual NDA.
Receiving Party's obligation to return or destroy all Confidential Information upon request or termination.
Optional restrictions on the Receiving Party competing with the Disclosing Party or soliciting its employees or customers using Confidential Information.
Equitable relief provision and damages. Typically more favourable to the Disclosing Party than in a mutual NDA.
Duration, survival period, governing law, jurisdiction, and standard boilerplate provisions.
Define Service, Software, Product, Support Hours, Severity Levels, Service Levels, and other key terms used throughout the agreement.
Detailed description of what is included (maintenance, patches, updates, support) and what is excluded (custom development, training, unsupported versions).
Uptime commitments, response time targets by severity, resolution time targets, and measurement methodology. Reference to SLA schedule.
Credit mechanism for SLA failures, credit calculation methodology, maximum credit cap, claim procedure, and whether credits are sole remedy.
Provider's security commitments: encryption, access control, vulnerability management, incident response, secure development practices. CRA-mandated for products with digital elements.
Obligation to notify of vulnerabilities, notification timeline (24h for actively exploited), content of notification, ENISA/CSIRT reporting. CRA Article 14 mandated.
Duration of security update support (minimum 5 years per CRA Article 13(8)), update delivery mechanism, free-of-charge requirement, backward compatibility.
Software Bill of Materials delivery obligation, format requirements (CycloneDX/SPDX), update frequency, accuracy warranty. CRA Article 13(1)(f) mandated.
agreement_structures.keywords: SBOM CRA article 13 software bill of materials

agreement_structures.section_description: Fee structure (fixed, usage-based, or tiered), payment terms, price adjustment mechanism, invoice requirements.
Aggregate liability cap, exclusion of consequential damages, uncapped carve-outs (IP indemnity, confidentiality breach, wilful misconduct, death/personal injury).
Mutual indemnification for third-party claims. Provider indemnifies for IP infringement and security breach. Customer indemnifies for misuse.
Initial term, renewal mechanism, termination for cause (with cure period), termination for convenience, insolvency termination, effects of termination.
Provider's obligations upon termination: data return/migration, transition assistance period, knowledge transfer, and continued service during transition.
Source code escrow for business continuity. Deposit requirements, update schedule, release conditions (insolvency, material breach, discontinuation).

agreement_structures.section_name: Governing Law, Dispute Resolution, and General Provisions
agreement_structures.section_description: Choice of law, jurisdiction or arbitration, confidentiality, force majeure, notices, assignment, entire agreement, and boilerplate.
Define Software, Documentation, Licensee, Licensor, License Key, Updates, Upgrades, and Permitted Use.
Scope of license: exclusive/non-exclusive, transferable/non-transferable, sublicensable/non-sublicensable, territory, field of use, number of seats/users/installations.
Prohibited activities: reverse engineering, decompilation, modification, sublicensing, service bureau use, benchmarking. Subject to statutory exceptions.
Licensor retains all IP rights. License does not transfer ownership. Feedback assignment. Trade secret acknowledgment.
List of open-source components, applicable licenses, copyleft warranty, compliance obligations.
Performance warranty (material conformance with documentation), defect warranty, warranty period, remedy (repair, replace, refund), disclaimer of implied warranties.
Aggregate cap, exclusion of consequential damages, carve-outs. Conspicuous formatting in common law jurisdictions.
License duration (perpetual or subscription), termination triggers, post-termination obligations (cease use, destroy copies).
Governing law, jurisdiction, export compliance, assignment, notices, entire agreement, severability.

agreement_structures.section_name: Scope and Relationship to Principal Agreement
agreement_structures.section_description: Identify the principal agreement this SLA supplements. Define precedence in case of conflict. Specify which services are covered.
Define Availability, Downtime, Scheduled Maintenance, Measurement Period, and methodology (synthetic monitoring, probe locations, measurement frequency).
Uptime percentages by environment (production, staging). Separate targets for different service components if applicable.
Severity classification scheme, response time targets per severity, escalation paths, support hours, and communication channels.
Events not counted as Downtime: scheduled maintenance, customer-caused issues, force majeure, third-party dependencies outside Provider's control.
Credit tiers based on availability percentage shortfall, maximum monthly credit cap, claim procedure, credit application method (offset against future invoices).
Monthly service reports, availability dashboards, regular review meetings, continuous improvement obligations, and escalation procedures.
Right to terminate the principal agreement if service levels are missed for a specified number of consecutive months or total months in a rolling period.
